turns.user_message: I was just sitting at the wooden table yesterday evening, staring at the steam rising off my coffee while the other chair stayed completely empty. It felt so heavy in that quiet room, like the air itself was holding its breath waiting for something that never came. I kept checking my phone out of habit, hoping for a ping or a text to break the silence, but nothing ever showed up. I just sat there feeling completely untethered, wondering why such a simple moment suddenly felt so isolating. It really threw off my whole evening.

That quiet moment at the table is honestly just the tip of the iceberg for how things have been lately. Every time we share a space now, there is this invisible wall that makes it impossible to actually connect. I catch myself rehearsing what to say before speaking, terrified that my words will just bounce off and disappear into the void. It is exhausting to constantly navigate around each other without ever truly touching base. I feel like I am walking on eggshells in my own space, waiting for the other shoe to drop.

The silence at that table has completely swallowed me whole, and I don't know how to breathe through it anymore. I look across the room and see a complete stranger wearing a familiar face, which makes my chest tighten with this heavy, suffocating dread. Every single interaction feels like a careful performance where I am desperately trying to keep the peace while losing myself in the process. I am so tired of pretending everything is fine when the foundation is clearly crumbling beneath my feet. It feels like I am drowning in plain sight, completely alone in a room full of shared history.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment.
